card.hook: A Web3 security extension that treats permit signatures as harmless signing, ignores ERC-1271 smart accounts, or only watches old proxy upgrade selectors will miss some of the highest-risk actions before wallet confirmation. MV3 makes this sharper: you cannot rely on remotely loaded logic or general blocking webRequest interception, so the classifier has to be packaged and contract-aware.
card.why_it_matters: If you are learning to build browser-extension security for smart-contract flows, this defines the minimum risk model your first prototype needs to classify correctly.
card.action: Write a one-page risk-spec note that lists exactly four event classes your extension will flag before wallet confirmation: ERC-20/ERC-721/ERC-1155 approvals including ERC-2612 permit, ERC-1271 signature validations, proxy admin changes, and proxy upgrade calls including upgradeToAndCall and upgradeAndCall.
